ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, plus more for deep-frying
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, plus more for deep-frying

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons minced leek (white part only) or scallion
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons minced leek (white part only) or scallion

ingredients.item: ½ cup (44 g) finely chopped straw or brown mushrooms
ingredients.original: ½ cup (44 g) finely chopped straw or brown mushrooms

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (22 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and chopped
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (22 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and chopped

ingredients.item: ½ cup (44 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and finely chopped
ingredients.original: ½ cup (44 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and finely chopped

ingredients.item: 1 cup (250 g) glass noodles, soaked until soft, drained, and roughly chopped
ingredients.original: 1 cup (250 g) glass noodles, soaked until soft, drained, and roughly chopped

ingredients.item: 1 cup (100 g) finely shredded taro
ingredients.original: 1 cup (100 g) finely shredded taro

ingredients.item: 1 cup (100 g) peeled and shredded carrot
ingredients.original: 1 cup (100 g) peeled and shredded carrot

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon sugar, plus 1 tablespoon for soaking rice paper
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon sugar, plus 1 tablespoon for soaking rice paper

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 20 rice paper or spring roll wrappers (6 inches/15 cm)
ingredients.original: 20 rice paper or spring roll wrappers (6 inches/15 cm)

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon lime juice, for soaking rice paper
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon lime juice, for soaking rice paper

ingredients.item: 2 cups (480 ml) water, to soften rice paper
ingredients.original: 2 cups (480 ml) water, to soften rice paper

ingredients.item: 1. Put mashed mung beans in a large bowl and set aside.
ingredients.original: 1. Put mashed mung beans in a large bowl and set aside.

ingredients.item: 2. Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a small skillet over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until slightly golden brown (about 1 minute). Remove from heat and transfer both vegetables and oil to bowl of mashed mung beans.
ingredients.original: 2. Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a small skillet over low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until slightly golden brown (about 1 minute). Remove from heat and transfer both vegetables and oil to bowl of mashed mung beans.

ingredients.item: 3. Add mushrooms, glass noodles, taro, carrot, salt, 1 teaspoon of sugar, stock powder, and pepper. Mix well and set aside.
ingredients.original: 3. Add mushrooms, glass noodles, taro, carrot, salt, 1 teaspoon of sugar, stock powder, and pepper. Mix well and set aside.

ingredients.item: 4. Prepare rice paper wrappers for rolling with the lime juice, water, and 1 tablespoon of sugar (here). Place a wrapper on a flat surface and add 1 tablespoon of filling closer to one end. Lift up that end, fold it, push the filling back a little bit, and then fold in both sides. Continue to roll tightly all the way to the other end. Repeat with remaining wrappers and filling.
ingredients.original: 4. Prepare rice paper wrappers for rolling with the lime juice, water, and 1 tablespoon of sugar (here). Place a wrapper on a flat surface and add 1 tablespoon of filling closer to one end. Lift up that end, fold it, push the filling back a little bit, and then fold in both sides. Continue to roll tightly all the way to the other end. Repeat with remaining wrappers and filling.

ingredients.item: 5. Line a medium saucepan with parchment paper to prevent rolls from picking up any burnt bits from the bottom. Fill with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil. Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying).
ingredients.original: 5. Line a medium saucepan with parchment paper to prevent rolls from picking up any burnt bits from the bottom. Fill with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil. Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying).

ingredients.item: 6. Fry rolls in batches of 5 to 8 over medium-low heat until golden brown (about 10 minutes). The rice paper might be sticky at first, so be sure to leave some space between each roll.
ingredients.original: 6. Fry rolls in batches of 5 to 8 over medium-low heat until golden brown (about 10 minutes). The rice paper might be sticky at first, so be sure to leave some space between each roll.

ingredients.item: 7. Using a wire spider or slotted spoon, transfer rolls to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 7. Using a wire spider or slotted spoon, transfer rolls to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 8. Serve within 1 hour of frying with vegan fish dipping sauce.
ingredients.original: 8. Serve within 1 hour of frying with vegan fish dipping sauce.
